title: Here's A Nut
content:
"Here's a nut, there's a nut;
        Hide it quick away,
        In a hole, under leaves,
        To eat some winter day.
        Acorns sweet are plenty,
        We will have them all:
        Skip and scamper lively
        Till the last ones fall."
meaning:
About: Louisa May Alcott was an American novelist. 
She is best known for the novel Little Women, published in 1868. This novel is loosely based on her childhood experiences with her three sisters.
